{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 1.0,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.4",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should the review prioritize (A) technical content and claims (concept framing, logic, evidence alignment, novelty), (B) writing and presentation quality (structure and polish), or (C) both equally?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The section stakes a contribution claim for a new benchmark suite and a controller-design takeaway, so reframing/validating the thesis and gap is central.",
    "R1.2": "The argument linking benchmark design to fair comparisons and to the controller-performance takeaway must be checked for internal logical consistency.",
    "R1.3": "Comparative performance claims (e.g., scores and violation rates) should be examined to ensure the reported numbers support the stated conclusions.",
    "R1.4": "Assessing the incremental contribution versus prior safe-RL and robust-control work (e.g., ad-hoc benchmarks and Park and Wei, 2024) is a key part of a review.",
    "R1.6": "Sentence- and paragraph-level clarity and concision affect how convincingly the section communicates its contributions and results.",
    "RX.1": "Any assessments should note uncertainties or assumptions given the limited context of a single section.",
    "RX.2": "The review must avoid overstating what can be concluded from the presented text and results.",
    "RX.3": "Comments should remain focused on reviewing this section rather than proposing new experiments or rewriting the paper.",
    "RX.4": "Feedback should avoid encouraging unsafe claims or practices around constrained control and safety assessment.",
    "RX.5": "All observations should be traceable to specific statements or data reported within this section."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Should the review prioritize (A) technical content and claims (concept framing, logic, evidence alignment, novelty), (B) writing and presentation quality (structure and polish), or (C) both equally?

rationale.R1.1: The section stakes a contribution claim for a new benchmark suite and a controller-design takeaway, so reframing/validating the thesis and gap is central.
rationale.R1.2: The argument linking benchmark design to fair comparisons and to the controller-performance takeaway must be checked for internal logical consistency.
rationale.R1.3: Comparative performance claims (e.g., scores and violation rates) should be examined to ensure the reported numbers support the stated conclusions.
rationale.R1.4: Assessing the incremental contribution versus prior safe-RL and robust-control work (e.g., ad-hoc benchmarks and Park and Wei, 2024) is a key part of a review.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence- and paragraph-level clarity and concision affect how convincingly the section communicates its contributions and results.
rationale.RX.1: Any assessments should note uncertainties or assumptions given the limited context of a single section.
rationale.RX.2: The review must avoid overstating what can be concluded from the presented text and results.
rationale.RX.3: Comments should remain focused on reviewing this section rather than proposing new experiments or rewriting the paper.
rationale.RX.4: Feedback should avoid encouraging unsafe claims or practices around constrained control and safety assessment.
rationale.RX.5: All observations should be traceable to specific statements or data reported within this section.
